---
title: "Local Pipeline Ops"
---

## Scope

`docs/pipeline/` contains local operational wrappers that support dataset acquisition and preprocessing around the main Cellerator pipeline. These scripts are useful, but they are not part of the packaged product surface.

This area currently provides:

- manifest-driven FASTQ acquisition helpers
- manifest-driven preprocessing wrappers
- local environment and manifest templates

## Important Boundary

These scripts are local-only:

- they are not wired into CMake
- they are not installed as product commands
- they may call external tools such as `prefetch`, `fasterq-dump`, `curl`, `wget`, or `pbrun`
- they are allowed to drive built Cellerator binaries indirectly, but product code must not depend on them

## Current Interface

The wrappers still use the older `CELLERATOR_PAPER_*` and `PAPER_*` environment-variable names because that was their original local workflow contract.

Current env template:

- `docs/pipeline/config/local.env.example`

Current entrypoints:

- `docs/pipeline/download/fetch_fastq.sh`
- `docs/pipeline/preprocess/run_unified_preprocess.sh`

## Download Flow

The download helper reads a TSV manifest with dataset identity, provider, accession or URL fields, and target-relative output paths. It defaults to dry-run mode so the command plan is visible before data movement starts.

Supported provider patterns currently include:

- `sra`
- `direct`
- `ena`
- `geo-http`

## Preprocess Flow

The preprocess helper reads a TSV manifest that pairs an input dataset root with a command template. It expands placeholders for data roots, work roots, references, and output paths, then either prints or runs the resulting command.

The manifest contract is intentionally command-template driven so different datasets can still pass through one log and provenance surface.

## Suggested Future Cleanup

If these wrappers become a permanent part of the broader Cellerator pipeline story, the next cleanup step should be renaming the `PAPER_*` environment surface to a neutral pipeline-specific contract. That rename is intentionally not done here because it would change operational behavior, not just documentation.
